title: Serotonin Syndrome
chief_complaint: My patient is agitated, confused, sweating profusely, and shaking. She just started a new antidepressant 3 days ago. She's on multiple psychiatric medications.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Hyperreflexia with clonus (sustained ankle clonus bilaterally). Hypertonia. Tremor. Ocular clonus (rapid eye movements).

hidden_data.medication_history: Fluoxetine 40mg daily (long-term). New medication: Buspirone 15mg TID started 3 days ago. Also takes Trazodone 50mg at bedtime.
hidden_data.cmp: Creatinine 1.4 (dehydration), CK 850 (rhabdomyolysis from muscle rigidity), normal LFTs

correct_diagnosis: Serotonin Syndrome (Severe) from SSRI + Buspirone + Trazodone Combination
key_learning_points: Serotonin syndrome = excess serotonergic activity in CNS from drug interactions. Classic triad: Neuromuscular excitation (clonus, hyperreflexia, rigidity) + Autonomic instability (hyperthermia, tachycardia, HTN, diaphoresis) + Mental status changes (agitation, confusion)

Hunter criteria: Clonus (spontaneous, inducible, or ocular) + Hyperreflexia + Tremor + Hypertonia + Temperature >38°C + Diaphoresis. This patient: Spontaneous clonus + ocular clonus + hyperreflexia + temp 39.5 = SEVERE serotonin syndrome
Culprit: SSRIs + MAOIs (most dangerous combo), SSRIs + other serotonergic drugs (Buspirone, Trazodone, Triptans, St. John's Wort, Fentanyl, Ondansetron, Linezolid)

Treatment: STOP all serotonergic drugs. Supportive care: IV fluids, cooling, benzodiazepines (Lorazepam for sedation and muscle relaxation). Cyproheptadine (5-HT2 antagonist) 12mg PO/NG then 2mg q2h if severe. ICU for severe (hyperthermia >41°C, rhabdomyolysis, DIC)
Differentiate from NMS: Serotonin = rapid onset (hours), clonus/hyperreflexia, dilated pupils, diarrhea. NMS = gradual (days), lead-pipe rigidity, bradyreflexia, normal pupils, constipation
